description: §40.9.5 — an `<auth role="X">` content block fires W-AUTH-CONTENT-NOT-GATED (warning). `<auth role>` gates only JS mount/behaviour (and only under --emit-per-route), NOT served HTML content: the gated markup is emitted verbatim into the payload and is visible to ALL viewers regardless of role.
rationale: §40.9.5: emit-html emits the `<auth>` element as a passthrough literal and renders its children as static markup; the reachability solver's per-role verdict is consumed only by the JS route-splitter, never by HTML emission. So a role-naming content gate leaks its markup — the lint fires one warning per site. SEVERITY ESCALATION (ss60 W2): fires only a WARNING — PA/user to rule whether a content-secrecy footgun should be a freeze-blocking ERROR.
